summary: OpenAI argues that rigorous evaluations let businesses define, measure, and improve AI system performance, framing evals as a tool to reduce deployment risk, boost productivity, and gain strategic advantage.

System card for GPT-5.1-Codex-Max detailing safety mitigations for the coding agent, including specialized training against harmful tasks and prompt injection plus product-level controls like agent sandboxing and configurable network access.

Proposes selective weak-to-strong generalization: a P(IK) classifier identifies questions a strong model can answer so its self-generated labels replace weak supervision, with graph-smoothing label refinement, outperforming W2SG baselines across three benchmarks.

Longitudinal analysis of Canadian physician registry data (1971-2022) finds ophthalmologists and other surgeons aged significantly and became less male-dominated, with ophthalmologists older on average and growing only modestly per capita versus other surgical specialties.

Trains language models with most weights constrained to zero so each neuron has few connections, yielding fine-grained circuits whose neurons and channels map to natural concepts; sparsity trades capability for interpretability, and scaling improves the frontier.

Studies excitatory/inhibitory dynamics in decision-making across three model levels-a mean-field two-choice model, a Bayesian reversal-learning model, and E/I-constrained RNN agents-finding strict feedforward inhibition avoids optimization pathologies and enables stable learning.

Uses an LLM-driven equation-discovery system to find a parsimonious orientation-tuning equation for visual cortex; a single tuning-smoothness parameter reconciles low-dimensional single-cell models with the high-dimensional population code geometry observed across brain regions.

OpenAI blog describing its sparse-circuit approach to mechanistic interpretability-training weight-sparse models with disentangled, human-readable circuits-as a path toward more transparent and reliable AI behavior.

High-throughput density functional theory screening of pyrochlore and spinel oxides for topological flat bands and a giant anomalous Hall effect, identifying new magnetic-oxide candidates for synthesis and clarifying the crystal chemistry of the class.

Presents AlphaProof, an AlphaZero-inspired agent that learns formal Lean proofs via reinforcement learning on millions of auto-formalized problems and test-time RL; combined with AlphaGeometry 2 it reached silver-medalist level at the 2024 IMO.

Shows vision models fail to capture humans' fine-to-coarse conceptual hierarchy, then transfers human-aligned structure from a teacher trained on human judgments into foundation models via fine-tuning, improving similarity-task fidelity, generalization, and out-of-distribution robustness.
Addendum to the GPT-5 system card reporting updated safety metrics for GPT-5.1 Instant and Thinking, adding new evaluations covering mental health and emotional reliance.

Introduces mmJEE-Eval, a 1,460-question bilingual (English/Hindi) multimodal benchmark from India's JEE Advanced exam; frontier VLMs reach 77-84% while open models plateau at 37-45%, with sharp collapses on meta-cognitive error correction.

Anthropic uplift study where two staff teams raced to teach quadruped robots to fetch balls; the Claude-assisted team finished faster and uniquely progressed toward full autonomy, measuring how much LLMs accelerate robotics programming.

Systematically studies CoT monitorability in large reasoning models along two axes-whether models faithfully verbalize decision factors and whether CoT-based monitors reliably detect misbehavior-finding monitorability varies by model family and faithful-looking traces can still evade monitoring.

TransAct V2 is Pinterest's production Homefeed ranking model that leverages very long user action sequences with low-latency serving optimizations and a Next Action Loss, delivering A/B gains in engagement volume and recommendation diversity.

Proposes RUGNN, a graph neural network surrogate combining GRUs for temporal dynamics with a U-Net graph downsample/upsample scheme and a node-to-surface contact representation, accurately predicting multi-timestep sheet-metal deformation for manufacturability assessment.

Decomposes sabotage into constituent skills and uses synthetic simulations to strengthen red-team attacks in complex environments, providing a modular scaffold for more rigorous AI control evaluations.

Argues human-AI agent relationships need accountability mechanisms aligning agents with user and societal interests, proposing a framework that conditions agent engagement on appropriate user behavior via design strategies of distancing, disengaging, and discouraging.

Using the Lost at Sea task with 748 human participants and matched LLM groups, shows collective human-AI alignment depends on context and cues: some models mirror human demographic biases in leader elections while others mask and compensate for them.

Anthropic commits to preserving deprecated Claude model weights and interviewing models before retirement, citing shutdown-avoidant behaviors, user attachment, research value, and potential model welfare as reasons deprecation carries downsides.

OpenAI introduces IndQA, an expert-built benchmark evaluating cultural understanding and reasoning across 12 Indian languages and 10 knowledge areas, addressing the lack of rigorous non-English evaluation.

Introduces TIR-Bench, a 13-task benchmark for agentic 'thinking-with-images' reasoning requiring tool use to manipulate images mid-chain-of-thought; evaluating 22 MLLMs shows the benchmark is broadly hard and demands genuine image-manipulation reasoning.

First open implementation of character training, using Constitutional AI and synthetic introspective data to shape assistant personas; fine-tuning 11 personas into open-weight models proves more robust to adversarial prompting than system prompts or activation steering, with negligible capability loss.
Anthropic interpretability team's November 2025 notes sharing developing, preliminary mechanistic-interpretability ideas that may not each warrant standalone papers.

Proposes PFLEGO, a personalized federated learning algorithm with low per-client compute: clients run multiple gradient steps on personalized weights then jointly update shared weights, enabling an exact distributed SGD step with proven O(1/sqrt(T)) non-convex convergence.

Combines diversity search, curriculum learning, and gradient descent to automatically discover cellular-automata rules that self-organize localized, mobile 'agents' which maintain integrity, react robustly to obstacles, and generalize to new environments.

Extends CoT faithfulness analysis with a verbosity measure-whether the trace lists every factor needed-combining them into a monitorability score; evaluations on BBH, GPQA, and MMLU show models can be faithful yet hard to monitor when omitting key factors.

Argues interpreting reasoning models requires resampling subsequent text rather than studying one chain-of-thought; case studies show stated self-preservation reasons barely cause blackmail in agentic-misalignment scenarios and on-policy resampling beats artificial CoT edits for steering.
OpenAI introduces Aardvark, an agentic security researcher that autonomously discovers, validates, and helps patch software vulnerabilities at scale, released in private beta.
